title: NOC 43202 – By-law Enforcement and Other Regulatory Officers | GTR Immigration
description: By-law Enforcement and Other Regulatory Officers under NOC 43202 are responsible for ensuring compliance with municipal and provincial laws and regulations. Classified under TEER 3, this occupation plays a key role in maintaining public safety, community standards, and regulatory enforcement across Canada. These professionals primarily work for municipal governments, provincial agencies, and regulatory authorities.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community and government servicesTEER3 – Occupations usually require a college diploma of less than two years or more than six months of on-the-job trainingMajor Group43 – Assisting occupations in education and in legal and public protectionSub-Major Group432 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionMinor Group4320 – Assisting occupations in legal and public protectionNOC Code43202

duties: By-law Enforcement and Regulatory Officers may perform:
Respond to complaints regarding stray or dangerous animals
Inspect commercial vehicles for compliance with safety regulations
Investigate violations of waste management by-laws
Enforce parking regulations on streets and municipal properties
Inspect vehicles for safety and compliance
employment_requirements: College diploma or courses in:Law and securityPublic administrationRegulatory complianceRelated administrative fields
Experience in regulatory or administrative roles may be required
Zoning inspectors may require:Civil engineering or architectural technology diplomaBuilding construction experience
Civil engineering or architectural technology diploma
Background checks and regulatory certification may be required depending on the province.
salary_prospects: By-law Enforcement and Regulatory Officers typically earn between:
$37,000 – $88,600 per year,depending on experience, specialization, and province.
labour_market_demand: By-law Enforcement and Regulatory Officers are employed in:
Opportunities in municipal and provincial government
Career growth into supervisory and regulatory management roles
Regulatory enforcement is essential to maintaining safety and compliance across Canadian communities.
pathways: By-law Enforcement and Other Regulatory Officers fall under TEER 3 skilled occupations, which may qualify for:
